디모데전서 2:1.content: I exhort therefore, first of all, that petitions, prayers, intercessions, and givings of thanks be made for all men:

디모데전서 2:2.content: for kings and all who are in high places, that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and reverence.

디모데전서 2:3.content: For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior,

디모데전서 2:4.content: who desires all people to be saved and come to full knowledge of the truth.

디모데전서 2:5.content: For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus,

디모데전서 2:6.content: who gave himself as a ransom for all, the testimony in its own times,

디모데전서 2:7.content: to which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle—I am telling the truth in Christ, not lying—a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.

디모데전서 2:8.content: I desire therefore that the men in every place pray, lifting up holy hands without anger and doubting.

디모데전서 2:9.content: In the same way, that women also adorn themselves in decent clothing, with modesty and propriety, not just with braided hair, gold, pearls, or expensive clothing,

디모데전서 2:10.content: but with good works, which is appropriate for women professing godliness.

디모데전서 2:11.content: Let a woman learn in quietness with full submission.

디모데전서 2:12.content: But I don’t permit a woman to teach, nor to exercise authority over a man, but to be in quietness.

디모데전서 2:13.content: For Adam was formed first, then Eve.

디모데전서 2:14.content: Adam wasn’t deceived, but the woman, being deceived, has fallen into disobedience;

디모데전서 2:15.content: but she will be saved through her childbearing, if they continue in faith, love, and sanctification with sobriety.
